```python
class TextEditor:

    def __init__(self):
        self.left = []
        self.right = []

    def addText(self, text: str) -> None:
        self.left.extend(list(text))

    def deleteText(self, k: int) -> int:
        deleted = min(k, len(self.left))
        self.left = self.left[:-deleted]
        return deleted

    def cursorLeft(self, k: int) -> str:
        moved = min(k, len(self.left))
        self.right = self.left[-moved:] + self.right
        self.left = self.left[:-moved]
        return ''.join(self.left[-min(10, len(self.left)):])

    def cursorRight(self, k: int) -> str:
        moved = min(k, len(self.right))
        self.left.extend(self.right[:moved])
        self.right = self.right[moved:]
        return ''.join(self.left[-min(10, len(self.left)):])

# Your TextEditor object will be instantiated and called as such:
# obj = TextEditor()
# obj.addText(text)
# param_2 = obj.deleteText(k)
# param_3 = obj.cursorLeft(k)
# param_4 = obj.cursorRight(k)
```

solution: 
```python
class TextEditor:

    def __init__(self):
        self.left = []
        self.right = []

    def addText(self, text: str) -> None:
        self.left.extend(list(text))

    def deleteText(self, k: int) -> int:
        deleted = min(k, len(self.left))
        self.left = self.left[:-deleted]
        return deleted

    def cursorLeft(self, k: int) -> str:
        moved = min(k, len(self.left))
        self.right = self.left[-moved:] + self.right
        self.left = self.left[:-moved]
        return ''.join(self.left[-min(10, len(self.left)):])

    def cursorRight(self, k: int) -> str:
        moved = min(k, len(self.right))
        self.left.extend(self.right[:moved])
        self.right = self.right[moved:]
        return ''.join(self.left[-min(10, len(self.left)):])

# Your TextEditor object will be instantiated and called as such:
# obj = TextEditor()
# obj.addText(text)
# param_2 = obj.deleteText(k)
# param_3 = obj.cursorLeft(k)
# param_4 = obj.cursorRight(k)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(k)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two lists to represent the text to the left and right of the cursor. It achieves O(k) time complexity per call by only manipulating the necessary elements. The space complexity is O(n) due to storing the text.
